steps.title: Identify the goal
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize how many participants selected an altered version. The relevant figure from the notes is 13.1% — that is the number that must appear prominently in the correct answer.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — When participants were asked to identify the correct version of Fred Flintstone, 86.9% selected the unaltered image.: Emphasizes participants who got it right, not those who chose an altered version. ✗
  • Choice B — A 2021 study showed participants an unaltered image of Fred Flintstone, who is a cartoon man in a spotted tunic, alongside two altered versions.: Describes the study setup and mentions no percentage at all. ✗
  • Choice C — Participants were asked to identify the correct version of Fred Flintstone, who is a cartoon man in a spotted tunic.: States the task but includes no data about altered-version selections. ✗
  • Choice D — When shown three versions of Fred Flintstone and asked to select the correct one, 13.1% of participants chose an altered version.: Puts the altered-version figure front and center, directly fulfilling the stated goal. ✓
That matches Choice D — When shown three versions of Fred Flintstone and asked to select the correct one, 13.1% of participants chose an altered version..
